zh.Heron Hall, East Horndon, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可易亞斯特 霍登
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jordan De Héron於大約1183年出生在英國諾圖波藍德時，他的父親Jordan De Héron18歲，母親Mrs Jordan De Heron II19歲。他和Lady Jordan De Heron至少有2個兒子。他去世於大約1216年在他的家鄉享年35歲。

zh-hans.Heron Hall, East Horndon, Essex, England: 英国英格兰艾可易亞斯特 霍登
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jordan De Héron于大约1183年出生在英国諾圖波藍德时，他的父亲Jordan De Héron18岁，母亲Mrs Jordan De Heron II19岁。他和Lady Jordan De Heron至少有2个儿子。他去世于大约1216年在他的家乡享年35岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Héron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Herron  .
French (Héron): nickname for a tall, thin person, thought to resemble a heron (Old French hairon).

variants.name: Herron
meanings: English: habitational name from Harome (North Yorkshire), from Old English (æt thǣm) hārum ‘at the (gray) stones’, dative plural of hār ‘gray, stone’.

English and Scottish: nickname probably for a tall, thin man with long legs, from Old French hairon ‘heron’, Anglo-Norman French heron, Middle English heiroun, heyron, herne, heron. Compare Hearn and Heron .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from (Le) Héron in France (Seine-Maritime).

nameMeanings.name: Jordan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a name given to a child of either sex baptized in holy water that was, purportedly at least, brought from the River Jordan, whose Hebrew name, ha-yarden, means ‘flowing down’. It was in this river that Christ was baptized by John the Baptist, and medieval pilgrims to the Holy Land usually tried to bring back a flask of its water with them. The modern given name is either a revival of this, or else a transferred use of the surname that was derived from the medieval given name. It is more popular as a boy's name in Britain and as a girl's name in the United States.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When Jordan De Héron was born about 1183, in Northumberland, England, his father, Jordan De Héron, was 18 and his mother, Mrs Jordan De Heron II, was 19. He had at least 2 sons with Lady Jordan De Heron. He died about 1216, in his hometown, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jordan De Héron est né environ 1183, à Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Jordan De Héron, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Mrs Jordan De Heron II, avait 19 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Lady Jordan De Heron. Il est décédé environ 1216, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 35 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Jordan De Héron(이)가 대략 1183년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jordan De Héron(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Jordan De Heron II(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Lady Jordan De Heron(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1216년 그의 고향에서 35세의 나이로 사망했다.
